frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: multivoxel pattern feedback used to causally manipulate a neural correlate.

frameworks.name: normative models of confidence
frameworks.one_line: view that confidence directly reflects reliability of perceptual computations.

frameworks.name: late-stage / dissociable confidence estimation
frameworks.one_line: confidence arises from a post-perceptual stage separable from sensory evidence.

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: framework underlying psychophysical controls for reporting strategy.

core_question: Does subjective confidence directly reflect perceptual reliability, or can it be dissociated from perceptual performance?
key_assumptions: multivoxel patterns in a frontoparietal network carry the neural correlate of confidence
DecNef induction acts selectively on the targeted multivoxel correlate
perceptual accuracy and confidence can be measured independently
manipulations: bi-directional DecNef induction of multivoxel confidence correlates (up vs down)
frontoparietal network targeting for the neurofeedback signature
key_findings: induced bi-directional confidence changes did not alter perceptual accuracy
psychophysical analyses rule out simple shifts in reporting strategy
interpretations: confidence is systematically dissociable from perceptual performance
results challenge normative accounts equating confidence with perceptual reliability
